characters.name: Archduck McGanza
characters.description: Archduck McGanzaRelativesCount DuckulaBehind the scenes informationVoiced byDavid JasonFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Archduck McGanzaisCount Duckula's great-uncle's great uncle. He is voiced byDavid Jasonand first appears in the episode "No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!". Igoris busy educating Count Duckula about his ancestry. Although Duckula doesn't want to hear about "his boring, old ancestors" in the beginning, Igor explains that Archduck McGanza was "far from boring" and that he was a "famous Egyptologist". Igor describes McGanza's lifelong ambition to find the Great Pyramid and his writings about his many "strange discoveries" there, including a "three-wheeled deck chair". But his strangest and best discovery was that of a great stone altar, which he actually discovered by mistake when he stumbled upon it in the darkness. As soon as McGanza had found the altar,Hoomite, a priest of the sun godRaand his assistant,Yoobee, who Igor says resembled "the walking dead", greet McGanza. However, Igor promptly concludes the story by saying that "the Archduck knew no more... knew no more than I do where theMystic Saxophonewas hidden."

characters.name: Aunt Lucrecia
characters.description: Aunt LucreciaAliasesAunty (referred to as byCount Duckula)RelativesCount Duckula (nephew)AffiliationAuntPhysical descriptionSpeciesVampire duckGenderFemaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byBrian TruemanFirst episode"Family Reunion"Aunt LucreciaisCount Duckula's aunt. She only appears in the episode "Family Reunion" and was voiced by the lateBrian Trueman, who also voicedNannyandRory McDuckula.

characters.name: Chatham, Cheetam and Runn
characters.description: Chatham, Cheetam and Runnare a firm of lawyers who have a role in the episode "Whodunnit?". They are assigned to handle the affairs of the seventh Duke of Mallardborough and send a letter toCount Duckula, inviting him toHenheim Palacefor the reading of the Duke's will.

characters.name: Count Duckula (character)
characters.description: Count DuckulaAliasesCount Duckula the 17th (full name)Duckyboos (byNanny)Milord (byIgor)Young Master (by Igor and Nanny)You Monster (byDr. Von Goosewing)You Fiend (by Dr. Von Goosewing)Feathered Prince of Evil (by Narrator)RelativesDon Diego(cousin)Rory McDuckulaAunt Lucrecia(aunt)Uncle Vlad(uncle)Physical descriptionSpeciesVampire DuckGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byDavid JasonFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Last episode"The Zombie Awakes"Count Duckula(full title:Count Duckula the 17th, as stated in the episode "The Count and the Pauper"), is the main protagonist ofCount Duckula. He is a short green duck with black parted hair, traditional vampire evening wear, complete with a cape. He has no fangs, although his more old-fashioned relatives do. As his name suggests, Count Duckula himself is very loosely based on the titular character of Bram Stoker'sDracula, and is essentially a subversion of the vampire trope. Duckula is a vegetarian vampire duck, coming into the world as an accident. Unlike his family and ancestors, he has no bloodlust, as when he was reincarnated, blood was omitted and replaced with ketchup. He is actually quite cowardly, with a distaste for blood and gore, toIgor's disappointment. As the theme song states, Duckula is rather zany. Energetic, good-natured, and ambitious, he is prone to short-lived obsessions that sometimes form the plots for episodes, such as attempting to become a blues musician in New Orleans, prospecting for gold, or becoming a cowboy or performer. He likes to make puns and jokes, and on occasion has the same sarcastic edge that Igor does. He has a very modern outlook, and despairs over the traditional vampire image he is expected to embody. In the episode "Family Reunion", he is willing to go as far as contactingDr Von Goosewingto help him destroy his own family. He hates living in adark, gloomy castle, and finds the behaviour of his servants to be depressing. Duckula frequently expresses frustration with Igor's lecturing and attempts to change him into a proper vampire, as well asNanny's simple-minded and clumsy nature. Still, he keeps them around, suggesting that he really does need them. Quite a few times he admits to missing Nanny while she's gone, and he always takes his employees back at the end of the day. Despite his positive qualities, Duckula is very immature, egotistical, and selfish, taking his lifestyle and employees for granted. He can be quite childish and rash, acting almost spoiled, despite his lack of riches. That also drives his avarice, a frequently occuring trait that has him and his staff scrambling for money. He does try to act refined and mature, which shows in his speech and fake attempts at modesty.

characters.name: Count Duckula's family
characters.description: This article lists the known family members ofCount Duckula.

characters.name: Danger Mouse (character)
characters.description: Danger Mouseis the titular character of theanimated TV seriesof the same name. He is voiced bySir David Jason, who also voicesCount Duckulaand Pierre. He is a white anthropomorphic mouse with yellow eyes and a dark pink nose. He wears a black eyepatch over one eye and a white suit with a circular red badge that says "DM" in yellow and a red belt with a yellow buckle. He appears as one of the circus audience members in "Sawdust Ring".

characters.name: Danger Mouse (character)
characters.description: Danger Mouseis the titular character of theanimated TV seriesof the same name. He is voiced bySir David Jason, who also voicesCount Duckulaand Pierre. He appears as one of the circus audience members in "Sawdust Ring".

characters.name: Dmitri and Sviatislav
characters.description: Dmitri and SviatislavPhysical descriptionSpeciesBatsGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Dmitri and Sviatislavare bats in a cuckoo clock that serve as comic relief. They first appear in the episode "No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!". Dmitri is a green bat with a yellow bow tie and a yellow hat and Sviatislav is a dark blue bat with a green hat, a thick, white moustache and a yellow scarf. Usually, their appearance is very brief with just enough time for a quick joke, and this is shown in their first appearance, where Dmitri asks Sviatislav: "What are they calling a pyramid with windows in it?" The answer is "A tomb with a view!" Dmitri and Sviatislav Fanart

characters.name: Don Diego
characters.description: Don DiegoRelativesCount Duckula (cousin)Biographical informationPlace of birthSpainPhysical descriptionSpeciesVampire duckGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"Vampire Vacation"Don DiegoisCount Duckula's cousin who first appeared in the episode "Vampire Vacation". He is described byIgoras a "pyromaniac", someone who likes setting fire to things. Although Don Diego thinks he is "good and kind and wonderful", the villagers believe he is dastardly and evil. One of the Spanish locals presents Don Diego as his "honourable highness, the dirty, diabolical, dastardly and disgusting, Don Diego!" Don Diego sets fire toCastle Duckula, and also reveals to Count Duckula that he chopped off all of the villagers except "the really fat ones", which he had been saving for that particular night, after which there would be "bingo to follow." Duckula refuses to take part in Don Diego's dastardly schemes and after an argument Don Diego only agrees to spare the villagers' lives if Duckula faces the famous bull,El Loco, in a classical bullfighting arena.

characters.name: Dr. Fazakerley Tyme
characters.description: Dr Fazakerley TymeBehind the scenes informationFirst episodeTransylvanian Homesick BluesDr. Fazakerley Tymeis the main antagonist of the fourth episode of Series One, 'Transylvanian Homesick Blues'. He's man in charge of "The Rollercoaster of Time" whenCount Duckula,NannyandIgordecided to visit the funfair together.

characters.name: Dr. Fazakerley Tyme
characters.description: Dr Fazakerley TymeBehind the scenes informationFirst episodeTransylvanian Homesick BluesDr. Fazakerley Tymeis the main antagonist of the fourth episode of Series One, 'Transylvanian Homesick Blues'. characters.name: Dr. Von Goosewing
characters.description: Dr. Von GoosewingAliasesThe Doctor/Doc (By Duckula)Goosewing (By Duckula, Igor)Cheekbox (By Nanny)Little sauceboat (By Nanny)That Lunatic (By Duckula)RelativesDr Von Gosling (great-uncle)Vanna Von Goosewing (niece)Physical descriptionSpeciesGooseGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byJimmy HibbertFirst episodeVampire VacationLast episodeAround the World in a Total DazeDr. Von Goosewingis the main antagonist ofCount Duckula.He's mad scientist, inventor, and a vampire hunter -- a spoof ofAbraham Van Helsingfrom Bram Stoker'sDracula. He relentlessly pursuesCount Duckula, unable to comprehend that he is harmless. Goosewing (By Duckula, Igor)Cheekbox (By Nanny)Little sauceboat (By Nanny)That Lunatic (By Duckula) Vanna Von Goosewing (niece) Von Goosewing is well defined by his bumbling, forgetful, and clumsy nature, as it is usually his downfall. He is unobservant, on occasion bumping into Duckula and conversing with him for several minutes without realizing to whom he is speaking, or forgetting to bring a weapon to even destroy him with. His realizations are often followed with the exclamation, “Duckula, you fiend!” He is rather easy-going when not furious with Duckula or caught up in some scheme, chuckling and generally talking to himself, often about how great he is. Von Goosewing has an ego that he doesn't try at all to hide, praising himself for his "brilliant ideas" and "sheer unadulterated geniusness". He does not often take the blame for his failures, rather, passing it on to Heinrich or simply blaming Duckula. Von Goosewing is fearful ofIgor, regarding him as "the most cunningest, and most cleverest being known to mankind".Nanny, while he does fear her strength, he recognizes as unintelligent, mocking and otherwise being sassy with her. At other times, their interactions have consisted of Nanny thinking Von Goosewing is Duckula's friend, or that he's flirting with her, both of which he is always confused about.

characters.name: Dr. Von Gosling
characters.description: Dr. Von GoslingRelativesDr Von Goosewing(grand nephew)AffiliationGreat UnclePhysical descriptionSpeciesGooseGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byJimmy HibbertFirst episodeDear DiaryDr. Von Goslingis the great uncle ofDr. Von Goosewing A vampire hunter, like his later relative, Von Gosling used his diary to record the invention of an anti-vampire device disguised as a camera.[1] In the episodeDear Diary(Season 1, Episode 17), Dr. Von Gosling appears similar to his grand nephew, but with clothing appropriate to the era. He also has more hair.

characters.name: Fang
characters.description: FangAliasesFluffykinsAffiliationCastle DuckulaPhysical descriptionSpeciesBatGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationFirst episodeBloodsucking Fruit Bats of the Lower Amazon Fangis a bat that lives atCastle Duckula. Fang makes his debut inBloodsucking Fruit Bats of the Lower Amazon, where, after his training byIgoras a blood-sucking bat is ruined by a preferred taste for choccy-biccies, Igor tricks Duckula into sending the castle to the Lower Amazon in order to find another bat to train Fluffy into a vampire bat.

characters.name: Gaston and Pierre
characters.description: Gaston and Pierre are a pair of French criminals who serve as occasional villains from Count Duckula (and anti-villains from Danger Mouse). Although they are both undeniably incompetent, the arrogant Gaston is ostensibly the brains of the outfit. Gaston is a tall, thin black stork, while Pierre is a stubby, short parakeet who sounds similar to Bluebottle from The Goon Show. The characters were adapted into non-bird form for yet another Cosgrove-Hall animated series - Victor and Hugo.

characters.name: Heinrich
characters.description: HeinrichisDr Von Goosewing's "assistant". The character never appears on screen in any of the episodes. However, Von Goosewing constantly refers and alludes to him so, as a result, there has been a lot of speculation about whether Heinrich had once really existed or not. One theory is that Heinrich is Von Goosewing's imaginary friend of sorts. He often speaks to Heinrich as if he were there -- for example, in the episode 'Castle Duckula: Open to the Public', Von Goosewing asks Heinrich what he thinks of his "greatest ever invention" (which is really another one of his contraptions). Although there is clearly no one there beside him, Von Goosewing tells Heinrich to calm down because he is too "enthusiastic". Another theory is that Heinrich was actually Von Goosewing's former assistant who was always complaining about his paltry wages. Von Goosewing mentions that Heinrich threatened to resign, but is still, supposedly, working with him. So the theory is that Heinrich might have quit, but Von Goosewing failed to realize it and believes he is still there.

characters.name: Hoomite and Yoobee
characters.description: Hoomite and YoobeeBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Hoomite and Yoobeeare characters who appear in the episode "No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!". Hoomite is a priest of the Egyptian sun god Ra and Yoobee is his assistant. They are the custodians of the Great Pyramid and of the legendary treasure hidden inside,The Mystic Saxophone.Igordescribes them as resembling "the walking dead". In the episode, they are seen greetingArchduck McGanzabut Igor does not know what happened next. WhenCount Duckula, Igor andNannyare exploring the pyramid themselves, Hoomite and Yoobee manage to capture Duckula. They tie him up to the sacrificial altar for trespassing into Upshee's secret tomb. When Nanny enters the tomb, Hoomite and Yoobee mistake her for Upshee. They start chanting "Upshee rises, Upshee rises." Duckula and Nanny are then able to escape.

characters.description: Hoomite and YoobeeBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Hoomite and Yoobeeare characters who appear in the episode "No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!". characters.name: Igor
characters.description: IgorAliasesMister Igor (byNanny)Physical descriptionSpeciesVultureGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byJack MayFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Last episode"The Zombie Awakes"Igoris the deuteragonist ofCount Duckula. He's a hunched-backed, anthropomorphic vulture, with a deep, slow voice. Igor acts in the manner of a traditional horror servant based on the stock character Igor, and adds a decidedly dark amount of powdered sugar to Duckula's breakfast in the morning. While living in the manor, Igor takes care of keeping the mansion running. He greatly dislikes his master's behaviour, and often encourages him to act in a far more ghastly manner. Although he will generally obey Duckula's specific orders (thwarting Duckula's attempt to stop the first Duckula becoming a vampire only because he was not explicitly ordered not to), he remains convinced that, if he could only talk Duckula into biting, maiming, torturing and otherwise brutalising people, he would return to the "good old days" of the previous counts who behaved more like evil vampires. Igor is very gloomy, stoic, and generally unanimated in terms of personality. He has a dry sense of humor and hates things like "bless you," "nice," "good," and "lovely" -- such words make him cringe, as he prefers the darker and more sinister side of life. He himself speaks in a formal manner, showing an advanced vocabulary. In "Hardluck Hotel", Igor goes on holiday to a rundown and dismal hotel, where he sits in complete darkness, enjoying the sounds of the damp creeping up the walls. Murder, death, and blood are topics he is very open and encouraging to. He admires evil of all kinds, even the little steps he thinks Duckula takes or the times he becomes an actual vampire causes him to be overjoyed. He is persistent, nostalgia ridden, and simply longing for the old days. Igor does have his moments of softness, sometimes caring for Duckula and Nanny more than he will admit, but at heart, he is evil. He doesn't show many signs of change, but at the very least has become more passive. Many years ago, when the current Duckula wasn't around, he seemed to have more expression to him, shown well in the episode "Dear Diary". Igor's conflict with Duckula is a large part of how he behaves now. On one hand, he is ever so disappointed in his master for not being like the previous incarnations. On the other, despite this, Igor is incredibly patient and loyal with the current Count Duckula. He is visibly upset whenever Duckula sells him off, especially so in "Arctic Circles". Perhaps he believes someday his attempts will succeed -- and either way, he has served the Duckulas for centuries, and that will not stop now. Interestingly, he does not desire to be the count, only a mere butler, demonstrated in "Prime-Time Duck". He is snarky with Nanny and Duckula, and can even become aggressive towards civilians. He is intelligent, though, and knows when to keep his mouth shut. Igor is very cunning, sly, an

characters.name: Krool
characters.description: KroolBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"A Fright at the Opera"KroolisThe Phantom of the Opera's assistant who appears in the episode "A Fright at the Opera". He andIgorhappen to be "old friends" from when they were still students. Krool is first introduced when the Phantom is looking for his cloak and mask at the very beginning of the episode. Krool assists his master, the Phantom, in fulfilling his dastardly deeds and almost everything else. The Phantom seems to enjoy berating or insulting Krool. For instance, he describes Krool as a "lazy dog" when he is looking for his cloak and mask. However, despite the Phantom's cruel remarks, Krool is very submissive and loyal to him.

characters.name: Krool
characters.description: KroolBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"A Fright at the Opera"KroolisThe Phantom of the Opera's assistant who appears in the episode "A Fright at the Opera". characters.name: Nanny
characters.description: NannyRelativesUnnamed sisterPrimrose (niece)Physical descriptionSpeciesHenGenderFemaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byBrian TruemanFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Last episode"The Zombie Awakes"Nannyis the tritagonist ofCount Duckula. She, as her name suggests, isCount Duckula's nanny, as well as housekeeper. She was voiced by the lateBrian Trueman, who also voicedRory McDuckulaandAunt Lucrecia. Nanny is a very large, strong, and yet unintelligent hen. She is the one who mistakes ketchup for blood in the reincarnation ritual, resulting in the current Count Duckula. She wears a maid-like outfit, with her right arm perpetually in a sling. The sling itself seems to have unlimited carrying capacity, as she is able to produce any number of items from it (in the style of Harpo Marx's recurring magic satchel joke). Nanny is very ditzy and motherly — a reoccurring gag is her inability to understand what the people around her are talking about. She often mixes up words and takes insult at conversations not directed at her. It is important to note that she is smarter than she appears. Even though she mixes up words, Nanny does show an understanding of what they mean. She is generally kind-hearted. Some of this may stem from her obliviousness, as she connects instantly with Mrs. Bludgeon, but is quick to be friendly even with people likeDr. Von Goosewing. Still, she has the will to stand up for herself, scolding others for being impolite and even telling them she will "smack their legs" for misbehaving. Nanny is rather emotional, being known to cry when upset or even happy. Nanny often thinks people are flirting with her. During the show's run, while there have been a few instances of characters in love with her, it is usually a misinterpretation on her part. At one point, Nanny does have her own little fan club.

characters.name: Narrator
characters.description: Thenarratoris a character who narrates every episode ofCount Duckula. He was voiced by the lateBarry Clayton.

characters.name: Penfold
characters.description: Ernest PenfoldisDanger Mouse's hamster assistant and best friend. Often mistaken for a mole (presumably on account of his spectacles), Penfold is actually a hamster. He is code-named the Jigsaw because, at the first sign of danger, he falls to pieces.

characters.name: Penfold
characters.description: Ernest PenfoldisDanger Mouse's hamster assistant and best friend. Often mistaken for a mole (presumably on account of his spectacles), Penfold is actually a hamster. He is code-named the Jigsaw because, at the first sign of danger, he falls to pieces.

characters.name: Rory McDuckula
characters.description: Rory McDuckulaAliasesJock (once byCount Duckula)RelativesCount DuckulaAffiliationUncleBiographical informationPlace of birthScotlandPhysical descriptionSpeciesVampire duckGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byBrian TruemanFirst episode"The Ghost of McCastle McDuckula"Rory McDuckulaisCount Duckula's father's father's uncle's uncle who first appeared in the episode "The Ghost of McCastle McDuckula". He owns Castle McDuckula which is mistaken for the Glens Sparrows Hotel. He was voiced by the lateBrian Trueman, who also voicedNannyandAunt Lucrecia. Rory is described byIgorto be the "Scourge of the Glens" and hopes that his influence will convince the current Duckula to behave more evil. Rory initially speaks in a heavy Scottish accent and dialect, but after a private talk with Igor he drops the dialect to be understood by Duckula. His castle is haunted by a ghost. Despite being described as evil by Igor, he is seen in the episode to be polite and courteous. He also refuses to biteDr. Von Goosewing's neck due to him being "the wrong collar size". Though, he does not like people staying in his castle without paying the bill.

characters.name: The Crow Brothers
characters.description: The Crow BrothersBehind the scenes informationFirst episode"No Sax Please, We're Egyptian!"Last episode"Venice a Duck Not a Duck"The Crow Brothersare the secondary antagonists ofCount Duckula. The Crow Brothers consist of Ruffles, Burt, and two others. Not much is known about them except that they are seen to be either hikers or professional burglars. They often try to burgleCastle Duckulabut are always foiled and end up with serious injuries.

characters.name: The Crow Brothers

characters.name: The Phantom of the Opera
characters.description: The Phantom of the OperaBehind the scenes informationFirst episodeA Fright at the OperaThe Phantom of the Operais the villain in the episode 'A Fright at the Opera'. With the aid of his assistantKrool, the Phantom intends to destroy the Paris opera house. The episode of 'Count Duckula', 'A Fright at the Opera', can be considered as another adaptation of Gaston Leroux's immensely successful novel, 'Le Fantôme de l'Opéra' (translated as 'The Phantom of the Opera'). In this episode, the Phantom seeks revenge for all those who had once mocked his musical works by blowing up the Paris opera house. The Phantom's plan to blow up the opera house involves the kidnapping of the prima donna of the opera, the great Elvira, along with his assistant Krool. He plans to kidnap and take her to his lair in the sewers so he can force her to sing one of his compositions. When Elvira hits a perfect 'Top C' note while singing his opera, the bomb inside the Phantom's great organ would detonate, thereby obliterating anyone who happens to be inside the opera house at the moment, includingCount Duckula,IgorandNanny.

characters.description: Uncle VladRelativesCount Duckula(nephew)AffiliationUnclePhysical descriptionSpeciesVampire birdGenderMaleBehind the scenes informationVoiced byJimmy HibbertFirst episode"Family Reunion"Uncle VladisCount Duckula's uncle. He only appears in the episode "Family Reunion".

characters.name: Vanna Von Goosewing
characters.description: Vanna Von GoosewingRelativesDr. Von Goosewing(uncle)AffiliationNiecePhysical descriptionSpeciesGoose(?)GenderFemaleVanna Von Goosewingis a character exclusive to the Count Duckula comics. She is the niece ofDr. Von GoosewingandCount Duckula's love interest. She does not appear in theoriginal cartoon. Vanna is shown as a feminine pink feathered bird, likely a goose. She has blonde hair, red dress, red shoes, and a green neck scarf. She is sometimes shown surrounded by hearts. She first appears in March 1989, Marvel Comics' Count Duckula issue #3 in the storyLove At First Bite.

characters.name: Vanna Von Goosewing

characters.name: Vatsa Mata-Hari
characters.description: Vatsa Mata-HariAffiliationSecret AgentBiographical informationPlace of birthThrushiaPhysical descriptionSpeciesThrush(?)GenderfemaleBehind the scenes informationFirst episodeCount Duckula (Marvel) #2Vatsa Mata-Hariis a reoccurring character in Marvel's Count Duckula Comics. She is the self proclaimed "greatest female spy" fromThrushia. LikeDr. Von Goosewing, she also usually trying to destroyCount Duckula. Vatsa has white feathers and black curly hair, and a small yellow beak. She does not have one standard outfit, as she wears different disguise in every appearance, though she typically wears red dresses. It is never stated what bird species she is, but she may be a Thrush, as she comes from Thrushia. Thrushia being portmanteau ofThrushandRussia.
